campaign: Stereoisomer Panel: H-1152 enantiomers vs LIMK2 + ROCK2
status: PARTIAL — API quota exhausted, docking pending re-run
rationale: H-1152 has 2 stereocenters (C4-CF3, C2-pyridine on piperidine ring). genmol_119 is the (R,S) enantiomer that scored +1.058 on LIMK2 and +0.509 on ROCK2. This panel enumerates all 4 stereoisomers to determine if stereochemistry drives binding affinity (eutomer vs distomer analysis).
limk2_results: PENDING — run scripts/dock_stereoisomers.py when API quota refreshes
rock2_results: PENDING — run scripts/dock_stereoisomers.py when API quota refreshes

preliminary_notes: genmol_119 (R,S) has strong LIMK2 binding (+1.058) — highest in entire GenMol campaign
ROCK2 binding is moderate (+0.509) — only 1/20 poses positive
Enantiomer (S,R) may show similar or different affinity — DiffDock is stereoaware
Diastereomers (R,R) and (S,S) have different 3D shapes — likely different binding
If >0.3 confidence difference between best and worst → significant stereopreference

ip_implications.if_strong_stereopreference: Pure eutomer (best enantiomer) would be ~2x more potent than racemic mixture. This is the esomeprazole strategy: same compound, better enantiomer, new patent. Commercial H-1152 is sold as racemate — nobody has characterized individual enantiomers.
ip_implications.if_weak_stereopreference: Racemic H-1152 (commercial) would have similar activity. Less IP differentiation, but easier to develop (no chiral synthesis needed).

enantiomer_pairs.(R,S) <-> (S,R): Mirror images — same physical properties, different binding
enantiomer_pairs.(R,R) <-> (S,S): Mirror images — same physical properties, different binding
enantiomer_pairs.(R,S) vs (R,R): Diastereomers — different physical properties (mp, solubility, etc.)

re_run_instructions.note: NVIDIA API quota refreshes daily. Re-run when 403 errors stop.
